Committee.CommitteeName: Joint Committee on the Justice System

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 134, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Marlon Anderson, a Democrat, represents St. Louis City (District 76) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2020. At the beginning of his third term in 2025, the House Democratic Caucus elected him as House Assistant Minority Floor Leader.
Born and raised in North St. Louis in the Baden and Walnut Park area, Anderson is a proud product of St. Louis Public Schools. He graduated from Gateway Institute of Technology in 2000, and obtained an associate’s degree from St. Louis Community College.
Anderson completed his bachelor’s degree in Mass Media from Clark Atlanta University, where he participated in NCAA Division II sports and was a member of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity. He completed a juris doctorate from Southern University Law Center in Baton Rouge, LA in 2011.
Anderson worked for the Louisiana Public Defender in the Juvenile Litigations Division, the St. Louis City Circuit Attorney’s Office, the St. Louis Public Defender’s Office, and the Missouri Board of Probation and Parole before opening his own law firm in 2013. He continues to provide zealous legal representation at the Law Office of Marlon S. Anderson.
Community engagement includes serving as chairman of the 27th Ward Safety Committee, Vice-President of the Northside Neighborhood Action Association, Co-Chair of a Voter Empowerment committee and as a member of the 27th Ward Housing Committee Board. Anderson is the co-owner of Brother’s Property Management, a lifetime member of St. Paul Saturdays Manhood and Development Group, and a member of the New Northside Missionary Baptist Church.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions for voter qualifications
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Modifies provisions relating to grand jury proceedings
Modifies provisions relating to the "Crime Victims' Compensation Fund" and establishes the "Victims of Domestic Violence Fund"
Designates November 26th each year as "Tina Turner Day"
Designates December 6th each year as "Reuben Shelton Day" in Missouri
Adds provisions relating to an offender attending a funeral of a relative
Designates the "Police Officer David Lee Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City
Requires the Highway Patrol to conduct an independent investigation any time a person under the age of fifty dies within a department of corrections facility
Modifies provisions for providing copies of medical records
Establishes provisions relating to plea agreements
Enacts provisions relating to law enforcement custodial interviews
Designates November 24th each year as "Frankie Muse Freeman Day" in Missouri
Modifies the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm
Modifies provisions relating to certain teacher retirement systems
Designates June 20th each year as John Goodman day
Specifies that offenders do not have to pay intervention fees for the first sixty days of probation, parole, or conditional release
Designates April 9th each year as "Clyde S. Cahill, Jr. Day"
Modifies the statute of limitations for certain debts
Enacts provisions authorizing an entertainment district
Grants Missouri Girls State the use of the House Chamber on Thursday, June 26, 2025, from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Restores voting rights to individuals on probation and parole
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
